title: Understanding the Moment of Inertia
text: To calculate the angular momentum, we first need to determine the moment of inertia of the blades. Each blade can be modeled as a uniform rod rotating about one end. The formula for the moment of inertia of a rod about one end is $$I=\frac{1}{3} m L^2$$. What is the moment of inertia for one blade?

title: Calculate Moment of Inertia for One Blade
text: Using the formula $$I=\frac{1}{3} m L^2$$, where $$m=6000$$ kg and $$L=30$$ $$m$$, calculate the moment of inertia for one blade.

title: Total Moment of Inertia for the Rotor Assembly
text: The rotor assembly consists of three blades. How do you calculate the total moment of inertia for the entire rotor assembly?

title: Calculate Total Moment of Inertia
text: Multiply the moment of inertia of one blade by the number of blades (3) to find the total moment of inertia.

title: Convert Rotation Rate to Radians per Second
text: The rotation rate is given in revolutions per minute $$\frac{rev}{min}$$. Convert this to radians per second $$\frac{rad}{s}$$ using the conversion factor: $$1$$ rev $$=$$ $$2\pi$$ rad and $$1$$ min $$=$$ $$60$$ s.

title: Calculate Angular Velocity
text: Convert $$20$$ $$\frac{rev}{min}$$ to $$\frac{rad}{s}$$ using the formula: angular velocity $$=$$ $$\frac{20\times2 \pi}{60}$$.

title: Calculate Angular Momentum
text: Now that you have the total moment of inertia and the angular velocity, use the formula for angular momentum: $$L=I \omega$$, where I is the total moment of inertia and omega is the angular velocity.

title: Final Calculation of Angular Momentum
text: Using $$L=I \omega$$, where $$I=540000$$ $$kg m^2$$ and $$omega=2.0944$$ $$\frac{rad}{s}$$, calculate the angular momentum.
